source_1: Northrop Grumman press release: first-ever GEO docking, 2020-02-25
source_2: McDowell GCAT satcat: phase ends DDate 2020 Feb 25 0715, Status DK (docked)
notes: Two sources agree to the hour. Excluded from scoring: MEV-1's own orbit-raising transit contaminates the window.

source_1: Northrop Grumman / Intelsat press release, 2021-04-12 1:34pm EDT
source_2: McDowell GCAT satcat: DDate 2021 Apr 12 1734, Status DK
notes: GCAT's 17:34 UTC = 1:34pm EDT exactly. Nearest over-bar altitude step is -20 days away and unrelated.

source_1: Northrop Grumman / Intelsat press release, 2021-04-12
source_2: McDowell GCAT satcat MEV-2 phase, DDate 2021 Apr 12 1734, Status DK
notes: Target stays passive during rendezvous so its own orbit barely changes. Structural blind spot, not a tuning issue.

source_1: U.S. Space Forces-Space confirmation, 2024-10-19 ~0430 UTC
source_2: Slingshot Aerospace + ExoAnalytic independent sensor networks tracked the debris cloud (57 pieces by 10-21)
notes: Signal is real but +10 days late (-29.87 km, 71x bar, 2024-10-29). NOTE: GCAT has NOT recorded a breakup phase for this object.

source_1: Intelsat newsroom press release: propellant leak late on 2019-04-07, total loss declared 04-18
source_2: Seradata independent analysis (disputed the stated cause: power loss, not fuel leak); SpaceNews
notes: DATE CORRECTED 07-22 from 04-09 to 04-07 (documented leak onset). Catalog step 04-09 = +2 day lag. Still caught.

source_1: SpaceNews: AMC-9 stopped responding 2017-06-17, energetic event ~0710 UTC
source_2: NASA Orbital Debris Quarterly News (ntrs.nasa.gov 20180001608) - independent of SES
notes: NASA and the trade press agree. Catalog step lands 2017-06-19, +2 days, inside the window.

source_1: SpaceNews / Orbital Sciences: command loss 2010-04-05, blamed on solar storm
source_2: USGS Geomagnetism Program: 'Space Weather Events of April 5, 2010 and the Galaxy 15 satellite', 0948 UT
notes: CRITICAL: our flagship 'went quiet' case, and the best-sourced miss in the set. Largest step 2.15 km (5x bar), +5 days late.

source_1: Northrop Grumman press release 2025-04-09 states IS-901 was pushed to graveyard before undocking
source_2: DOWNGRADED 07-22: no second independent source. Trade coverage all derives from the same release; the date is catalog-derived
notes: +413 km over 03-31..04-03, drift -0.016 -> -5.26 deg/day. Physically unambiguous, but only one documentary source and we supplied the date.

source_1: Inferred from catalog step + published MEV-1 conops
source_2: No press release names this specific burn
notes: MEV-1 flying the docked stack toward the GEO belt. Inclination -0.111 deg in one step.

source_1: 'AMC-9, A Long Road to Graveyard Orbit' (ResearchGate) documents the move as ~November 2017
source_2: Exact burn date is catalog-derived; source gives only the month
notes: Executed as a staircase of ~10-13 km burns Oct-Nov 2017, +351 km total.

source_1: McDowell GCAT geotab: GEO/D at 36106x36137 km (~+335 km above belt)
source_2: No operator announcement found; date is catalog-derived

source_1: McDowell GCAT geotab: GEO/D at 36114x36159 km
source_2: No operator announcement found. GCAT confirms identity chain: S33153 launched as Protostar 1, renamed Oct 2009
notes: +112 km on 05-04 then ~10 km steps through 05-16; +349 km total.

source_1: Inferred from signature: -0.047 deg inclination correction with near-zero altitude change
source_2: No per-maneuver disclosure exists for any GEO operator
notes: Contradicts the 'altitude is blind to GEO station-keeping' hypothesis.

source_1: Same signature class
source_2: No per-maneuver disclosure exists
notes: Weakest true positive in the GEO set at 2x bar.

source_1: Same signature class
source_2: No per-maneuver disclosure exists

source_1: McDowell GCAT geotab: GEO/ID, inclination free-drifting 1.215 -> 2.697 deg over 18 months
source_2: DOWNGRADED 07-22: McDowell's classification derives from the same public catalog we measure. Not independent
notes: Null object. 1604 epochs, max step 0.51 km. Anchors the noise floor.

source_1: Space-Track SATCAT DECAY field (authoritative US government record)
source_2: RE-CHECKED 2026-07-22 night: GCAT satcat.tsv still Status O, no decay date (file stamp 'Updated 2026 Jul 17 2011:55', unchanged). NB the earlier note cited rcat.tsv - that is GCAT's SUBORBITAL ROCKET catalog, not reentries; wrong reasoning, same conclusion. satcat.tsv is the correct source and still lags.
notes: Present in SupGP through 07-21/2157Z, absent from 07-22/0200Z. Operator withdrew ephemeris before our first catalog-bearing snapshot. Re-check rcat in a week to promote.

source_1: Space-Track: launched 2026-06-25; catalog shows +5.17 km/day at 371 km
source_2: DOWNGRADED 07-22: SpaceX FCC conops describes orbit-raising generically, never per-satellite. The classification is our inference
notes: Top flag of the 0200Z snapshot at 9.3x its bar. The one class of event our archive can currently score.

source_1: Space-Track: launched 2026-02-20; catalog shows -0.23 km/day at 467 km
source_2: No public document confirms or denies a maneuver
notes: Top flag of the 0750Z snapshot at 11.2x its bar. Unresolvable from public sources.

source_1: Our detector: persistent top OneWeb flag, 5-5.9x bar across all snapshots
source_2: No public per-maneuver disclosure exists for OneWeb; zero OneWeb decay records 2026-06-01..07-22

source_1: Signature inference from GP_HISTORY: paired opposite-sign longitude-drift corrections ~1 day apart with inclination held at noise (<0.0058 deg, the measured null p99) - the textbook east-west station-keeping pattern
source_2: No per-maneuver disclosure exists for any GEO operator, so no documented EW burn is obtainable from public sources. Marked assumed for the same reason as the NS rows.
notes: Paired with 09-07 (+0.1785 deg/day) and 09-08 (-0.1974 deg/day): start-drift then stop-drift. Inclination untouched.

source_2: No per-maneuver disclosure exists for any GEO operator, so no documented EW burn is obtainable from public sources. Marked assumed for the same reason as the NS rows.
notes: Paired 02-23 (+0.2321) / 02-28 (-0.2668 deg/day). Same bird as the MEV-2 docking row, which altitude missed - EW burns it sees.
